title: Crossover from Rabi oscillations to adiabatic population switching in the Faraday optical control of quantum dot spins
authors: Jan M. Kaspari, Zhe Xian Koong, Dorian A. Gangloff, Michał Gawełczyk, Doris E. Reiter
abstract: Stimulated Raman transitions in Faraday geometry allow for simultaneous single-shot qubit readout and qubit control. It involves driving an unbalanced $Λ$ system via an auxiliary excited state. Due to the simultaneous driving of both transitions with unequal detuning, the resulting time-dependent Stark shift gives rise to additional resonance conditions beyond the conventional picture. We identify a distinct regime in which repeated passages through avoided crossings lead to step-like population inversion arising from Landau-Zener-Stückelberg interference. By changing the detuning beatnote, we demonstrate a controlled continuous crossover from Rabi-like oscillations to adiabatic population switching. These findings establish the oscillating Stark shift as a mechanism for engineering and controlling spin dynamics in Faraday geometry.
summary: This paper explores how to control spin dynamics in quantum dot qubits using optical fields in Faraday geometry. The authors demonstrate a novel mechanism where changing the laser detuning beatnote allows a controlled crossover from standard Rabi oscillations to highly efficient, step-like adiabatic population switching. This establishes the oscillating Stark shift as a powerful tool for engineering spin resonance conditions.
main_problem: The paper investigates the controlled transition between Rabi-like coherent oscillations and adiabatic population switching when controlling quantum dot spins using Faraday geometry optical fields.
main_result: By tuning the detuning beatnote, the system can be engineered to exhibit a controlled crossover from Rabi oscillations to step-like population inversion via Landau-Zener-Stückelberg interference.
method: The dynamics are analyzed by solving the Liouville-von Neumann equation for the density matrix, comparing results from the full three-level model with effective two-level spin models.
model_or_system: The system is a singly charged quantum dot spin coupled to negative trion states, modeled as a three-level $\Lambda$ system driven by Raman laser fields in Faraday geometry. The dynamics are governed by a time-dependent Hamiltonian.
key_observables: Population dynamics of spin states, coherent rotations, population inversion, and the differential AC Stark shift.
important_parameters: Detuning beatnote ($\delta$), imbalance ($\eta$), detuning ($\Delta$), and Zeeman splitting ($\omega_e$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the dipole and rotating wave approximations, and the effective model assumes $\Delta \gg \Omega$.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate energy level diagrams comparing Voigt and Faraday geometries, and show population dynamics for different harmonic orders ($n=1, 3$) and the $n=0$ case, demonstrating the crossover.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from establishing the physical setup and Hamiltonian to deriving effective models, analyzing resonance conditions for different harmonic orders ($n$), and finally demonstrating the controlled crossover from Rabi to adiabatic switching in the $n=0$ case.
why_interesting: This work provides a detailed theoretical framework for using time-dependent, engineered couplings (the oscillating Stark shift) to precisely control quantum state transfer in solid-state spin qubits, a key area for quantum computing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Energy level diagrams of the spin system in a) Voigt geometry and b) in Faraday geometry, including the spin states and the optical transitions indicated by arrows.
FIG. 3. Population dynamics of spin states for 𝑛= 1 in the balanced case 𝜂= 1 with Ω = 23.87 GHz and ℏ𝛿= 10.75 µeV (2.6 GHz).

FIG. 2. Population dynamics of the spin states for Ω = 23.87 GHz for a) 𝑛= 1 with ℏ𝛿= 8.79 µeV (2.13 GHz) and b) 𝑛= 3 with ℏ𝛿= 2.93 µeV (0.71 GHz). c) Period of the oscillations (red) and the maximal achievable population inversion (blue) for orders 𝑛= 1, . . . , 5. The lines are just a guide to the eye.

FIG. 4. Population dynamics of spin states (top panels) and instantaneous diabatic energy difference ℏ𝜔diff (bottom) for 𝑛= 0 at a) diabatic limit: ℏ𝛿= 17.11 µeV (4.14 GHz), b) intermediate step-like dynamics: ℏ𝛿= 0.39 µeV (0.10 GHz), and c) adiabatic limit: ℏ𝛿= 19.75 neV (4.77 MHz).
